top_strategic.title: Propose new lab: badge-prediction-and-prompt-tuning
top_strategic.evidence_summary:
Badge prediction achieves F1=0.99 but 5 badges are consistently missed; prompt engineering fixes exist but signal a systematic gap in training data or model alignment. A dedicated lab should own the data corpus for badge taxonomy, run prompt iteration experiments, and maintain production classification quality across all badge types.

Evidence: prompt_engineer (5 badges consistently missed, add positive examples), production_auditor (DaaS badge prediction F1=0.99 but precision/recall imbalance on edge cases), general-purpose gap_finder (missing outcome data)
top_strategic.recommended_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py badge-prediction-and-prompt-tuning — then customize config. Suggested researchers: label-quality-engineer, prompt-optimization-specialist, annotation-corpus-manager

proposal.rationale: Badge prediction achieves F1=0.99 but 5 badges are consistently missed; prompt engineering fixes exist but signal a systematic gap in training data or model alignment. A dedicated lab should own the data corpus for badge taxonomy, run prompt iteration experiments, and maintain production classification quality across all badge types.
proposal.evidence: prompt_engineer (5 badges consistently missed, add positive examples), production_auditor (DaaS badge prediction F1=0.99 but precision/recall imbalance on edge cases), general-purpose gap_finder (missing outcome data)
proposal.business_context: Badge prediction powers the DaaS product and feeds all downstream models (viral shot, sponsorship intent, player metrics); this lab owns data quality and prompt iteration to push F1 toward 0.995+ while eliminating the 5 missed badge types.

draft_config.description: Badge prediction achieves F1=0.99 but 5 badges are consistently missed; prompt engineering fixes exist but signal a systematic gap in training data or model alignment. A dedicated lab should own the data corpus for badge taxonomy, run prompt iteration experiments, and maintain production classification quality across all badge types.
draft_config.business_context: Badge prediction powers the DaaS product and feeds all downstream models (viral shot, sponsorship intent, player metrics); this lab owns data quality and prompt iteration to push F1 toward 0.995+ while eliminating the 5 missed badge types.

top_strategic.plausibility_note: Badge prediction is a standard classification task with readily available user data; prompt tuning via LLM APIs is straightforward if training data exists.

multi_model_check.change_mind: Show root cause analysis of the 5 consistently missed badges. Are they edge cases, labeling errors, or genuinely rare patterns? Validate that prompt engineering fixes generalize to held-out data, not just the observed gap. Provide before/after F1 scores on independent test set.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: High F1 (0.99) can mask class imbalance problems. If the 5 missed badges are rare, the overall metric is insensitive to failure mode. Also missing: cost of false positives in badge prediction—does 0.99 F1 mean acceptable precision for downstream systems?

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Propose new lab: badge-prediction-and-prompt-tunin'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py badge-prediction-and-prompt-tuning — then customize config. Suggested researchers: label-quality-engineer, prompt-optimization-specialist, annotation-corpus-manager

top_strategic.title: Propose new lab: performance-sequence-dynamics
top_strategic.evidence_summary:
Multiple labs identify shot sequencing and rally structure as abstracted away from analysis, yet viral shot detection and player consistency metrics suggest fine-grained temporal patterns matter. A dedicated lab should reconstruct shot sequences as first-class data objects and model how rally progression drives both performance outcomes and viral potential.

Evidence: gap_finder (shot sequence/rally structure abstracted), shot_scientist (overhead clips show 5.12x viral lift), correlation_scout (consistency↔composure r=0.90 suggests temporal coherence), wildcard synthetic player training
top_strategic.recommended_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py performance-sequence-dynamics — then customize config. Suggested researchers: temporal-sports-analytics-researcher, sequence-modeling-engineer, rally-reconstruction-specialist

proposal.rationale: Multiple labs identify shot sequencing and rally structure as abstracted away from analysis, yet viral shot detection and player consistency metrics suggest fine-grained temporal patterns matter. A dedicated lab should reconstruct shot sequences as first-class data objects and model how rally progression drives both performance outcomes and viral potential.
proposal.evidence: gap_finder (shot sequence/rally structure abstracted), shot_scientist (overhead clips show 5.12x viral lift), correlation_scout (consistency↔composure r=0.90 suggests temporal coherence), wildcard synthetic player training
proposal.business_context: Coaching OS and Synthetic Player Training both need shot-by-shot replay logic; modeling rally sequences unlocks personalized drill generation and opponent prediction with far higher fidelity than aggregate stats.

draft_config.description: Multiple labs identify shot sequencing and rally structure as abstracted away from analysis, yet viral shot detection and player consistency metrics suggest fine-grained temporal patterns matter. A dedicated lab should reconstruct shot sequences as first-class data objects and model how rally progression drives both performance outcomes and viral potential.
draft_config.business_context: Coaching OS and Synthetic Player Training both need shot-by-shot replay logic; modeling rally sequences unlocks personalized drill generation and opponent prediction with far higher fidelity than aggregate stats.

top_strategic.plausibility_note: Requires time-series sports data (moderately available via APIs like ESPN/Statsbomb) but performance metric definition and dynamics modeling adds complexity.

multi_model_check.change_mind: The finding identifies a gap (shot sequencing abstracted away) but doesn't quantify impact. Show: (a) how much performance degrades without sequence context, (b) examples where sequence matters vs. doesn't, (c) whether viral shot detection actually requires sequence or just clip-level features. Current evidence is observational, not causal.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: Conflates 'multiple labs identify X' with 'X is important.' Convergence suggests consensus but not necessity. Missing: baseline performance comparison (sequence-naive vs. sequence-aware) and user studies showing sequence context improves real-world decisions.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Propose new lab: performance-sequence-dynamics'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py performance-sequence-dynamics — then customize config. Suggested researchers: temporal-sports-analytics-researcher, sequence-modeling-engineer, rally-reconstruction-specialist

top_strategic.title: Propose new lab: sponsorship-intent-detection
top_strategic.evidence_summary:
Four separate opportunity findings converge on sponsorship: 97% of clips have zero brand detection, 4 equipment categories are whitespace, 194 player-brand pairs exist at 3+ co-occurrence threshold, and Gear Forensics suggests reverse detection is viable. This deserves a dedicated corpus of brand-player-equipment co-occurrence patterns and intent signals.

Evidence: pickle-idea-lab opportunity_scout (sponsorship whitespace 4182 clips 97%), opportunity_scout (4 equipment categories ZERO brands), opportunity_scout (194 player-brand pairs), wildcard Gear Forensics
top_strategic.recommended_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py sponsorship-intent-detection — then customize config. Suggested researchers: brand-safety-analyst, co-occurrence-pattern-miner, sponsorship-economics-modeler

proposal.rationale: Four separate opportunity findings converge on sponsorship: 97% of clips have zero brand detection, 4 equipment categories are whitespace, 194 player-brand pairs exist at 3+ co-occurrence threshold, and Gear Forensics suggests reverse detection is viable. This deserves a dedicated corpus of brand-player-equipment co-occurrence patterns and intent signals.
proposal.evidence: pickle-idea-lab opportunity_scout (sponsorship whitespace 4182 clips 97%), opportunity_scout (4 equipment categories ZERO brands), opportunity_scout (194 player-brand pairs), wildcard Gear Forensics
proposal.business_context: Brands need to know which equipment categories and player archetypes drive authentic endorsement moments; this lab turns raw clip data into sponsorship intelligence products and feeds Gear Forensics service.

draft_config.description: Four separate opportunity findings converge on sponsorship: 97% of clips have zero brand detection, 4 equipment categories are whitespace, 194 player-brand pairs exist at 3+ co-occurrence threshold, and Gear Forensics suggests reverse detection is viable. This deserves a dedicated corpus of brand-player-equipment co-occurrence patterns and intent signals.
draft_config.business_context: Brands need to know which equipment categories and player archetypes drive authentic endorsement moments; this lab turns raw clip data into sponsorship intelligence products and feeds Gear Forensics service.

top_strategic.plausibility_note: Needs intent inference from sparse signals (sponsorship data, engagement patterns); requires ML model training and labeled data that's hard to source quickly.

multi_model_check.change_mind: This is the strongest finding. Four independent signals + 97% zero brand detection + named whitespace (4 equipment categories, 194 player-brand pairs) = concrete, measurable gaps. Confidence justified by convergence and specificity.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: Doesn't establish *why* brands are absent: poor annotation, truly absent in source data, or brand mentions aren't salient to the task? Also: 97% zero detection could reflect annotation schema (if sponsors weren't labeled) rather than real opportunity. Audit the labeling process.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Propose new lab: sponsorship-intent-detection'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py sponsorship-intent-detection — then customize config. Suggested researchers: brand-safety-analyst, co-occurrence-pattern-miner, sponsorship-economics-modeler

top_strategic.title: Propose new lab: player-longitudinal-cohort-studies
top_strategic.evidence_summary:
72 players with 5+ clips and 27 new records per cycle create a rare longitudinal dataset. Rather than treating clips as static snapshots, a dedicated lab should track individual player trajectories—skill evolution, consistency drift, composure under pressure—across time and match context.

Evidence: pickle-idea-lab opportunity_scout (72 players with 5+ clips), change_tracker (27 new records), correlation_scout (skills.consistency ↔ skills.composure), general-purpose wildcard Talent Scout Automation
top_strategic.recommended_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py player-longitudinal-cohort-studies — then customize config. Suggested researchers: longitudinal-sports-scientist, cohort-trajectory-modeler, player-development-analyst

proposal.rationale: 72 players with 5+ clips and 27 new records per cycle create a rare longitudinal dataset. Rather than treating clips as static snapshots, a dedicated lab should track individual player trajectories—skill evolution, consistency drift, composure under pressure—across time and match context.
proposal.evidence: pickle-idea-lab opportunity_scout (72 players with 5+ clips), change_tracker (27 new records), correlation_scout (skills.consistency ↔ skills.composure), general-purpose wildcard Talent Scout Automation
proposal.business_context: Talent Scout Automation and Coaching OS both need player growth signals; longitudinal tracking unlocks early-stage scouting (who is trending up) and personalized development roadmaps.

draft_config.description: 72 players with 5+ clips and 27 new records per cycle create a rare longitudinal dataset. Rather than treating clips as static snapshots, a dedicated lab should track individual player trajectories—skill evolution, consistency drift, composure under pressure—across time and match context.
draft_config.business_context: Talent Scout Automation and Coaching OS both need player growth signals; longitudinal tracking unlocks early-stage scouting (who is trending up) and personalized development roadmaps.

top_strategic.plausibility_note: Longitudinal studies require 6+ months of historical cohort data and complex statistical analysis; 30 days insufficient for data collection and validation.

multi_model_check.change_mind: 72 players + 5+ clips is promising but modest for longitudinal work. Show: (a) growth trajectory—will 72 become 200+?, (b) inter-clip time intervals (are these weekly? yearly?), (c) preliminary cohort analysis demonstrating that treating clips as related (not static) improves prediction. Without trajectory data, this is potential, not validated opportunity.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: Assumes longitudinal signal exists without evidence. Many players may have static skill/persona across clips, making temporal modeling unnecessary. Also: 'dedicated lab' is resource-heavy—missing is cost-benefit vs. incremental gains from simpler time-series features.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Propose new lab: player-longitudinal-cohort-studie'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py player-longitudinal-cohort-studies — then customize config. Suggested researchers: longitudinal-sports-scientist, cohort-trajectory-modeler, player-development-analyst

top_strategic.title: No clear persona gaps detected across 2 labs
top_strategic.evidence_summary: No heavy category concentration or recurring DA themes warrant new personas right now. Will re-check as more runs accumulate.
top_strategic.recommended_action: Continue daily runs — patterns will emerge.
top_strategic.plausibility_note: Analysis/documentation work requires no external APIs or data collection; mostly internal discovery and reporting that one person can complete in 30 days.

multi_model_check.change_mind: Null finding with appropriate epistemic humility ('will re-check as more runs accumulate'). Absence of evidence isn't evidence of absence, but the reasoning is sound: no concentration or recurring themes = no clear need yet. This is good auditing discipline.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: Depends on coverage: were both labs equally exhaustive in persona discovery? If one lab was shallow, absence of gaps there isn't meaningful. Also missing: operational definition of 'heavy concentration'—what threshold would trigger a persona proposal?

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'No clear persona gaps detected across 2 labs'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Continue daily runs — patterns will emerge.

top_data.title: Convergent signal across general-purpose+pickle-idea-lab: 'Synthetic Player Training: AI Opponent Sim'
top_data.evidence_summary: Lab 'general-purpose' (wildcard) found 'Synthetic Player Training: AI Opponent Sim'. Lab 'pickle-idea-lab' (opportunity_scout) independently found 'Endorsement intelligence: 194 player-brand pairs with 3+ co-occurrences'. Similarity: 0.442. When multiple labs converge on a theme, it's a much stronger signal.
top_data.recommended_action: This theme deserves dedicated investigation. Consider a focused lab or a cross-lab follow-up.

raw_data.finding_a: Synthetic Player Training: AI Opponent Sim
raw_data.finding_b: Endorsement intelligence: 194 player-brand pairs with 3+ co-occurrences

proposal.rationale: Multiple labs identify shot sequencing and rally structure as abstracted away from analysis, yet viral shot detection and player consistency metrics suggest fine-grained temporal patterns matter. A dedicated lab should reconstruct shot sequences as first-class data objects and model how rally progression drives both performance outcomes and viral potential.
proposal.evidence: gap_finder (shot sequence/rally structure abstracted), shot_scientist (overhead clips show 5.12x viral lift), correlation_scout (consistency↔composure r=0.90 suggests temporal coherence), wildcard synthetic player training
proposal.business_context: Coaching OS and Synthetic Player Training both need shot-by-shot replay logic; modeling rally sequences unlocks personalized drill generation and opponent prediction with far higher fidelity than aggregate stats.

draft_config.description: Multiple labs identify shot sequencing and rally structure as abstracted away from analysis, yet viral shot detection and player consistency metrics suggest fine-grained temporal patterns matter. A dedicated lab should reconstruct shot sequences as first-class data objects and model how rally progression drives both performance outcomes and viral potential.
draft_config.business_context: Coaching OS and Synthetic Player Training both need shot-by-shot replay logic; modeling rally sequences unlocks personalized drill generation and opponent prediction with far higher fidelity than aggregate stats.

top_20.plausibility_note: Requires time-series sports data (moderately available via APIs like ESPN/Statsbomb) but performance metric definition and dynamics modeling adds complexity.

multi_model_check.change_mind: The finding identifies a gap (shot sequencing abstracted away) but doesn't quantify impact. Show: (a) how much performance degrades without sequence context, (b) examples where sequence matters vs. doesn't, (c) whether viral shot detection actually requires sequence or just clip-level features. Current evidence is observational, not causal.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: Conflates 'multiple labs identify X' with 'X is important.' Convergence suggests consensus but not necessity. Missing: baseline performance comparison (sequence-naive vs. sequence-aware) and user studies showing sequence context improves real-world decisions.

top_20.title: Propose new lab: sponsorship-intent-detection
top_20.evidence_summary:
Four separate opportunity findings converge on sponsorship: 97% of clips have zero brand detection, 4 equipment categories are whitespace, 194 player-brand pairs exist at 3+ co-occurrence threshold, and Gear Forensics suggests reverse detection is viable. This deserves a dedicated corpus of brand-player-equipment co-occurrence patterns and intent signals.

Evidence: pickle-idea-lab opportunity_scout (sponsorship whitespace 4182 clips 97%), opportunity_scout (4 equipment categories ZERO brands), opportunity_scout (194 player-brand pairs), wildcard Gear Forensics
top_20.recommended_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py sponsorship-intent-detection — then customize config. Suggested researchers: brand-safety-analyst, co-occurrence-pattern-miner, sponsorship-economics-modeler

proposal.rationale: Four separate opportunity findings converge on sponsorship: 97% of clips have zero brand detection, 4 equipment categories are whitespace, 194 player-brand pairs exist at 3+ co-occurrence threshold, and Gear Forensics suggests reverse detection is viable. This deserves a dedicated corpus of brand-player-equipment co-occurrence patterns and intent signals.
proposal.evidence: pickle-idea-lab opportunity_scout (sponsorship whitespace 4182 clips 97%), opportunity_scout (4 equipment categories ZERO brands), opportunity_scout (194 player-brand pairs), wildcard Gear Forensics
proposal.business_context: Brands need to know which equipment categories and player archetypes drive authentic endorsement moments; this lab turns raw clip data into sponsorship intelligence products and feeds Gear Forensics service.

draft_config.description: Four separate opportunity findings converge on sponsorship: 97% of clips have zero brand detection, 4 equipment categories are whitespace, 194 player-brand pairs exist at 3+ co-occurrence threshold, and Gear Forensics suggests reverse detection is viable. This deserves a dedicated corpus of brand-player-equipment co-occurrence patterns and intent signals.
draft_config.business_context: Brands need to know which equipment categories and player archetypes drive authentic endorsement moments; this lab turns raw clip data into sponsorship intelligence products and feeds Gear Forensics service.

top_20.plausibility_note: Needs intent inference from sparse signals (sponsorship data, engagement patterns); requires ML model training and labeled data that's hard to source quickly.

multi_model_check.change_mind: This is the strongest finding. Four independent signals + 97% zero brand detection + named whitespace (4 equipment categories, 194 player-brand pairs) = concrete, measurable gaps. Confidence justified by convergence and specificity.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: Doesn't establish *why* brands are absent: poor annotation, truly absent in source data, or brand mentions aren't salient to the task? Also: 97% zero detection could reflect annotation schema (if sponsors weren't labeled) rather than real opportunity. Audit the labeling process.

top_20.title: Propose new lab: player-longitudinal-cohort-studies
top_20.evidence_summary:
72 players with 5+ clips and 27 new records per cycle create a rare longitudinal dataset. Rather than treating clips as static snapshots, a dedicated lab should track individual player trajectories—skill evolution, consistency drift, composure under pressure—across time and match context.

Evidence: pickle-idea-lab opportunity_scout (72 players with 5+ clips), change_tracker (27 new records), correlation_scout (skills.consistency ↔ skills.composure), general-purpose wildcard Talent Scout Automation
top_20.recommended_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py player-longitudinal-cohort-studies — then customize config. Suggested researchers: longitudinal-sports-scientist, cohort-trajectory-modeler, player-development-analyst

proposal.rationale: 72 players with 5+ clips and 27 new records per cycle create a rare longitudinal dataset. Rather than treating clips as static snapshots, a dedicated lab should track individual player trajectories—skill evolution, consistency drift, composure under pressure—across time and match context.
proposal.evidence: pickle-idea-lab opportunity_scout (72 players with 5+ clips), change_tracker (27 new records), correlation_scout (skills.consistency ↔ skills.composure), general-purpose wildcard Talent Scout Automation
proposal.business_context: Talent Scout Automation and Coaching OS both need player growth signals; longitudinal tracking unlocks early-stage scouting (who is trending up) and personalized development roadmaps.

draft_config.description: 72 players with 5+ clips and 27 new records per cycle create a rare longitudinal dataset. Rather than treating clips as static snapshots, a dedicated lab should track individual player trajectories—skill evolution, consistency drift, composure under pressure—across time and match context.
draft_config.business_context: Talent Scout Automation and Coaching OS both need player growth signals; longitudinal tracking unlocks early-stage scouting (who is trending up) and personalized development roadmaps.

top_20.plausibility_note: Longitudinal studies require 6+ months of historical cohort data and complex statistical analysis; 30 days insufficient for data collection and validation.

multi_model_check.change_mind: 72 players + 5+ clips is promising but modest for longitudinal work. Show: (a) growth trajectory—will 72 become 200+?, (b) inter-clip time intervals (are these weekly? yearly?), (c) preliminary cohort analysis demonstrating that treating clips as related (not static) improves prediction. Without trajectory data, this is potential, not validated opportunity.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: Assumes longitudinal signal exists without evidence. Many players may have static skill/persona across clips, making temporal modeling unnecessary. Also: 'dedicated lab' is resource-heavy—missing is cost-benefit vs. incremental gains from simpler time-series features.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Propose new lab: player-longitudinal-cohort-studie'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py player-longitudinal-cohort-studies — then customize config. Suggested researchers: longitudinal-sports-scientist, cohort-trajectory-modeler, player-development-analyst

top_20.recommended_action: Continue daily runs — patterns will emerge.
top_20.plausibility_note: Analysis/documentation work requires no external APIs or data collection; mostly internal discovery and reporting that one person can complete in 30 days.

multi_model_check.change_mind: Null finding with appropriate epistemic humility ('will re-check as more runs accumulate'). Absence of evidence isn't evidence of absence, but the reasoning is sound: no concentration or recurring themes = no clear need yet. This is good auditing discipline.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: Depends on coverage: were both labs equally exhaustive in persona discovery? If one lab was shallow, absence of gaps there isn't meaningful. Also missing: operational definition of 'heavy concentration'—what threshold would trigger a persona proposal?

all_findings.title: Convergent signal across general-purpose+pickle-idea-lab: 'Synthetic Player Training: AI Opponent Sim'
all_findings.evidence_summary: Lab 'general-purpose' (wildcard) found 'Synthetic Player Training: AI Opponent Sim'. Lab 'pickle-idea-lab' (opportunity_scout) independently found 'Endorsement intelligence: 194 player-brand pairs with 3+ co-occurrences'. Similarity: 0.442. When multiple labs converge on a theme, it's a much stronger signal.
all_findings.recommended_action: This theme deserves dedicated investigation. Consider a focused lab or a cross-lab follow-up.

raw_data.finding_a: Synthetic Player Training: AI Opponent Sim
raw_data.finding_b: Endorsement intelligence: 194 player-brand pairs with 3+ co-occurrences

all_findings.title: Propose new lab: badge-prediction-and-prompt-tuning
all_findings.evidence_summary:
Badge prediction achieves F1=0.99 but 5 badges are consistently missed; prompt engineering fixes exist but signal a systematic gap in training data or model alignment. A dedicated lab should own the data corpus for badge taxonomy, run prompt iteration experiments, and maintain production classification quality across all badge types.

Evidence: prompt_engineer (5 badges consistently missed, add positive examples), production_auditor (DaaS badge prediction F1=0.99 but precision/recall imbalance on edge cases), general-purpose gap_finder (missing outcome data)
all_findings.recommended_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py badge-prediction-and-prompt-tuning — then customize config. Suggested researchers: label-quality-engineer, prompt-optimization-specialist, annotation-corpus-manager

proposal.rationale: Badge prediction achieves F1=0.99 but 5 badges are consistently missed; prompt engineering fixes exist but signal a systematic gap in training data or model alignment. A dedicated lab should own the data corpus for badge taxonomy, run prompt iteration experiments, and maintain production classification quality across all badge types.
proposal.evidence: prompt_engineer (5 badges consistently missed, add positive examples), production_auditor (DaaS badge prediction F1=0.99 but precision/recall imbalance on edge cases), general-purpose gap_finder (missing outcome data)
proposal.business_context: Badge prediction powers the DaaS product and feeds all downstream models (viral shot, sponsorship intent, player metrics); this lab owns data quality and prompt iteration to push F1 toward 0.995+ while eliminating the 5 missed badge types.

draft_config.description: Badge prediction achieves F1=0.99 but 5 badges are consistently missed; prompt engineering fixes exist but signal a systematic gap in training data or model alignment. A dedicated lab should own the data corpus for badge taxonomy, run prompt iteration experiments, and maintain production classification quality across all badge types.
draft_config.business_context: Badge prediction powers the DaaS product and feeds all downstream models (viral shot, sponsorship intent, player metrics); this lab owns data quality and prompt iteration to push F1 toward 0.995+ while eliminating the 5 missed badge types.

all_findings.plausibility_note: Badge prediction is a standard classification task with readily available user data; prompt tuning via LLM APIs is straightforward if training data exists.

multi_model_check.change_mind: Show root cause analysis of the 5 consistently missed badges. Are they edge cases, labeling errors, or genuinely rare patterns? Validate that prompt engineering fixes generalize to held-out data, not just the observed gap. Provide before/after F1 scores on independent test set.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: High F1 (0.99) can mask class imbalance problems. If the 5 missed badges are rare, the overall metric is insensitive to failure mode. Also missing: cost of false positives in badge prediction—does 0.99 F1 mean acceptable precision for downstream systems?

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Propose new lab: badge-prediction-and-prompt-tunin'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py badge-prediction-and-prompt-tuning — then customize config. Suggested researchers: label-quality-engineer, prompt-optimization-specialist, annotation-corpus-manager
